visual: Same "Marbles" data. Scale of 1 still showing from previous interaction (24 tick marks visible).
guide: "Scale of 1 works, but that's a lot of lines. You learned bigger scales are usually easier to read."
preview_displays: Bars appear with ⚠️ indicators. Jar A (7) between 0 and 10. Jar C (19) between 10 and 20.
guide_3: "Hmm. Scale of 10 fits... but there are only 3 marks on the axis. Look at those warnings. Can you tell the exact value of where those bars end? Someone looking at our graph without the data table would need to guess how many marbles are shown."
visual_highlight: ⚠️ on Jar A with message: "7 won't land exactly on a line or in the middle"
guide_4: "For big data, we'd pick Scale of 10 anyway—it's the easiest to read because we don't want too many lines on the axis. But these numbers are small and it's hard to find the exact location of the bar on the graph. We can do better."
key_teaching_point: "When data is small, you have room to find a scale where everything lands exactly."
